themes.exampleQuote: The bartenders are top class — friendly, knowledgeable about the cocktails, and they project this vibe that makes me want to come back.
The cacio e pepe pizza is a must.
Waited 10 minutes for a sip of Jäger and whiskey.
Trans folks beware — the staff didn't ask my friend's name and instead used the deadname on their credit card.

notable_quote: Statistically, I'm bound to have less than a perfect experience here, but it hasn't happened — maybe this place is the bee's knees.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the bartenders' warmth and cocktail knowledge, with specific dishes like the cacio e pepe pizza earning repeat mentions; the friction points are operational — a slow drink on one visit and a significant staff inclusivity misstep that surfaced in the review record.
quarter_narrative: Lorelei entered Spring 2026 with 392 Google reviews, a 72% five-star share, and a last review just twelve days out — a steady cadence that holds its rank without drama. The conversion path is airtight: hours, phone, and website all present on Google, which is the one axis where the entire Signal Pittsburgh family is already maxed out. What Lorelei can't claim this quarter is Instagram momentum — four posts in 30 days, zero Reels, and a 0.79% engagement rate put it well behind the family median and nowhere near Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings, which is running away with the Pittsburgh Businesses lead on social signal alone. The path from Ones to Watch to a higher tier runs directly through the Instagram gap; the cocktail credibility and staff charisma already in the review record give it real material to work with.
tldr_read: Airtight conversion path and loyal reviewers, near-silent Instagram with zero Reels in the last 30 days — Ones to Watch, #2 in Pittsburgh Restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The review floor is stable and the conversion infrastructure is complete; the ceiling is capped by an Instagram signal that is producing at roughly a third of the family median, with Reels entirely absent from the recent record.

playbook.headline: Start Reels before the next issue
playbook.action: Zero Reels in 30 days against a family median that favors video — one behind-the-bar Reel per week closes the gap fast.

playbook.headline: Put the cocktail catalog on Google
playbook.action: 291 photos across 7 categories but the visual signal sits 28 points below the family median — owner-submitted cocktail and pizza close-ups are the fastest fix.

playbook.headline: Address the inclusivity review publicly
playbook.action: A single unaddressed negative review about deadnaming is visible in the five most recent — a direct, specific owner response signals accountability to future reviewers.
